owasp_top10: A09:2021 - Security Logging and Monitoring Failures
mitre_attack: T1070.001 - Indicator Removal: Clear Windows Event Logs
T1562.001 - Impair Defenses: Disable or Modify Tools
cwe: CWE-807 - Reliance on Untrusted Inputs in a Security Decision

threat.title: Payment Transaction Non-Repudiation Failure Enabling Chargeback Fraud
threat.description: When payment systems lack cryptographic proof of transaction authorization (digital signatures, tamper-proof audit trails, HSM-backed signing), users can falsely deny making legitimate purchases, leading to fraudulent chargebacks. This vulnerability exploits the inability to prove transaction authenticity, costing merchants billions annually through 'friendly fraud' and first-party misuse. Without non-repudiation controls, merchants cannot definitively prove a customer authorized a payment, resulting in automatic disputes favoring consumers.

attack.scenario:
1. Customer makes legitimate $2,500 purchase on e-commerce site using saved payment method
2. Payment gateway processes transaction successfully without cryptographic signature or customer re-authentication
3. Transaction logs capture timestamp, IP address, and session ID but lack digital signature proving customer intent
4. 30 days later, customer initiates chargeback claiming 'unauthorized transaction' or 'did not authorize'
5. Merchant provides transaction logs, but without cryptographic proof (digital signature, HSM-signed receipt), evidence is deemed insufficient
6. Card network rules favor consumer; merchant loses both merchandise ($2,500) and chargeback fees ($15-100), plus inventory
7. Customer retains product and receives refund (friendly fraud completed)
8. Attacker shares technique on social media (TikTok 'chargeback hacks'), encouraging mass exploitation
9. Merchant experiences 40% increase in chargebacks over 6 months, triggering monitoring programs and higher processing fees
prerequisites: Payment system lacks digital signature or cryptographic proof of transaction authorization
No requirement for transaction-level customer authentication (e.g., biometric, OTP for high-value purchases)
Audit logs are not tamper-proof or cryptographically sealed
Transaction receipts not digitally signed by HSM or secure enclave
Merchant relies solely on IP logs, cookies, or session data as proof of authorization

cve_references.description: Non-repudiation failures are architectural weaknesses rather than specific CVEs

real_world_breaches.impact: 79% of merchants reported first-party fraud in 2024 (up from 34% in 2023); friendly fraud costs merchants $132 billion annually
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Social media platforms popularized 'chargeback hacks' teaching consumers to dispute legitimate transactions. Merchants lacked cryptographic proof to counter false claims.

real_world_breaches.impact: Chargeback rates rose 8% globally in first three quarters of 2024; dispute rates spiked 78% year-over-year in Q3. Chargebacks projected to cost eCommerce $33.79B in 2025, reaching $41.69B by 2028.
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Insufficient non-repudiation controls allowing consumers to falsely claim unauthorized transactions. Merchants lose $4.61 for every dollar of fraud.

real_world_breaches.impact: Finance director authorized $499,000 payment after fraudulent Zoom call using deepfake CFO. In separate 2024 case, $25M transferred via deepfake video conference.
real_world_breaches.root_cause: Lack of cryptographic transaction signing and multi-party authorization. Verbal/video authorization insufficient without digital signatures.

security_research.title: 2025 AFP Payments Fraud and Control Report
security_research.key_finding: 79% of organizations reported attempted or actual fraud attacks in 2025. Audit trail deficiencies cited as primary enabler of payment disputes.

security_research.title: Visa Compelling Evidence 3.0 Framework
security_research.key_finding: New rules require merchants to provide cryptographic evidence of authorization for disputed transactions. Traditional logs (IP, session) deemed insufficient.

security_research.title: Mastercard Ethoca Chargeback Projection
security_research.key_finding: Chargebacks projected to rise 40% from 2023 to 2026, driven by first-party fraud and inadequate non-repudiation controls.

mitigations.title: Implement RSA Digital Signatures for High-Value Transactions
mitigations.description: Cryptographically sign all payment transactions using RSA-2048 or higher asymmetric encryption. Customer's private key signs transaction details (amount, merchant, timestamp), creating irrefutable proof of authorization. Signature is verified using customer's public key and stored immutably in audit logs.
iso27001_controls: A.10.1.1 - Policy on the use of cryptographic controls
nist_csf: DE.CM-1 - The network is monitored to detect potential cybersecurity events
PR.PT-4 - Communications and control networks are protected
validation_steps: Generate RSA-2048 key pair for test customer
Sign test transaction with customer private key
Verify signature validation succeeds with correct public key
Verify signature validation fails with incorrect public key or tampered data
Test timestamp expiration (reject signatures older than 5 minutes)
Verify audit log contains signature and signed data
Simulate chargeback dispute and verify evidence package generation

mitigations.title: Blockchain-Based Immutable Audit Trail for Payment Transactions
mitigations.description: Store cryptographic hashes of all payment transactions on a private or consortium blockchain (e.g., Hyperledger Fabric, Ethereum private chain). Each transaction record includes hash of payment details, customer authorization proof, timestamp, and previous block hash, creating tamper-proof audit trail. Smart contracts enforce transaction validation rules.
iso27001_controls: A.18.1.4 - Privacy and protection of personally identifiable information
nist_csf: PR.DS-6 - Integrity checking mechanisms are used to verify software, firmware, and information integrity
PR.PT-1 - Audit/log records are determined, documented, implemented, and reviewed
DE.AE-3 - Event data are collected and correlated from multiple sources and sensors
validation_steps: Deploy Hyperledger Fabric network with payment-audit chaincode
Test recordPaymentTransaction function with sample payment
Query blockchain to retrieve transaction record
Verify transaction integrity using verifyTransactionIntegrity
Attempt to modify stored record (should fail - immutability)
Generate complete customer transaction history
Test dispute evidence generation with blockchain proof
Benchmark transaction throughput (target: 1000+ TPS)

mitigations.title: HSM-Backed Transaction Signing with Azure Payment HSM
mitigations.description: Use Hardware Security Modules (HSM) to sign payment transactions with FIPS 140-2 Level 3 certified tamper-resistant hardware. HSM generates and stores cryptographic keys that never leave the secure hardware boundary. Each transaction receives PKCS#11 compliant digital signature, providing legal-grade non-repudiation meeting PCI-DSS and SOX requirements.
iso27001_controls: A.10.1.1 - Policy on the use of cryptographic controls
nist_csf: PR.DS-5 - Protections against data leaks are implemented
PR.DS-6 - Integrity checking mechanisms are used to verify information integrity
PR.PT-4 - Communications and control networks are protected
validation_steps: Provision Azure Payment HSM instance (FIPS 140-2 Level 3 certified)
Generate RSA-2048 signing key in HSM with non-exportable flag
Test transaction signing with sample payment data
Verify signature using HSM verify operation
Attempt to export private key (should fail - tamper-proof)
Benchmark signing performance (target: 1000+ signatures/second)
Store HSM-signed transaction in audit database
Generate dispute evidence package with HSM proof
Validate compliance with PCI-DSS Requirement 3.6 (key management)
Test key rotation procedure without service disruption

indicators: Sudden spike in chargeback disputes (>10% increase month-over-month)
Multiple disputes from same customer ID within short timeframe
High-value transactions followed by disputes within 30-90 day window
Disputes claiming 'unauthorized transaction' for accounts with strong authentication history
Pattern of disputes after product delivery confirmation
Chargebacks submitted immediately after chargeback window opens
Customer transaction history shows normal usage before dispute
Missing or incomplete transaction authorization signatures in audit logs
Audit log gaps or inconsistencies around disputed transactions
Social media mentions of merchant name + 'chargeback' or 'dispute'

log_queries.query:
index=payments sourcetype=stripe_events
| search event_type="charge.dispute.created"
| eval dispute_age=now()-_time
| bin _time span=1d
| stats count as dispute_count, avg(amount) as avg_dispute_amt, values(customer_id) as customers by _time
| where dispute_count > 10
| eval alert_severity=case(
    dispute_count > 50, "critical",
    dispute_count > 25, "high",
    dispute_count > 10, "medium"
  )
| table _time dispute_count avg_dispute_amt customers alert_severity
| sort -dispute_count

// Detect payment dispute patterns indicating non-repudiation failures
PaymentEvents
| where EventType == "ChargebackCreated" or EventType == "DisputeInitiated"
| where TimeGenerated >= ago(30d)
| extend DisputeReason = tostring(Properties.dispute_reason)
| summarize 
    DisputeCount = count(),
    TotalDisputeAmount = sum(todouble(Properties.amount)),
    UniqueCustomers = dcount(CustomerId),
    ReasonBreakdown = make_set(DisputeReason)
  by bin(TimeGenerated, 1d), MerchantId
| where DisputeCount > threshold (10) // Configurable threshold
| extend PercentIncrease = (DisputeCount - prev(DisputeCount)) / prev(DisputeCount) * 100
| where PercentIncrease > 10 // 10% increase triggers alert
| project 
    Date = TimeGenerated,
    MerchantId,
    DisputeCount,
    TotalDisputeAmount,
    UniqueCustomers,
    PercentIncrease,
    ReasonBreakdown
| order by DisputeCount desc

event.category: "payment" AND event.type: "dispute"
  AND event.outcome: "created"
  AND @timestamp >= "now-30d"
| stats 
    count() as dispute_count,
    sum(payment.amount) as total_disputed,
    cardinality(customer.id) as unique_customers,
    values(dispute.reason) as dispute_reasons
  by merchant.id, bucket(@timestamp, 1d)
| where dispute_count > 10
| eval chargeback_rate = dispute_count / total_transactions * 100
| where chargeback_rate > 0.9
| sort -dispute_count

tools.name: Stripe Radar for Fraud Detection
tools.usage: Detects high-risk transactions based on velocity, device fingerprinting, and behavioral patterns. Integrates with Stripe API to flag potential friendly fraud.

tools.name: Verifi CDRN (Cardholder Dispute Resolution Network)
tools.usage: Real-time alerts when cardholders initiate disputes, allowing merchants to issue refunds before chargeback is filed.

tools.name: Ethoca Alerts
tools.usage: Mastercard's dispute notification system providing early warning of potential chargebacks.

tools.name: Sift Science
tools.usage: Machine learning platform detecting friendly fraud patterns across transaction history, device fingerprints, and user behavior.

compliance: ESIGN Act 2000 (Electronic Signatures in Global and National Commerce Act)
ISO27001 (A.12.4.1 - Event Logging, A.12.4.2 - Log Protection)
